articles.title: Taara Brings Fiber-Optic Speeds to Open-Air Laser Links​
articles.summary: Taara started as a Google X moonshot spin-off aimed at connecting rural villages in sub-Saharan Africa with beams of light. articles.title: This Student-Built EV Focuses on Repairability
articles.summary: At first glance, the Aria EV doesn’t look much different from any other student-built electric prototype—no different from the battery-powered cars built by engineering students from dozens of universities every year. articles.title: From TV Repairman to Electromagnetic Compatibility Expert
articles.summary: No one had very high career aspirations for teenager David A. Weston—except for Weston himself. Growing up in London, he scored low on the U.K. national assessment test given to students finishing primary school. articles.title: The Millisecond That Could Change Cancer Treatment
articles.summary: Inside a cavernous hall at the Swiss-French border, the air hums with high voltage and possibility. articles.title: Laser-Based 3D Printing Could Build Future Bases on the Moon
articles.summary: Through the Artemis Program, NASA hopes to establish a permanent human presence on the Moon in its southern polar region.
